problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Neuroinflammation. Neuroinflammation reduction via microglial targeting directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: EEG/MEG signal analysis with 5 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
